settings.title: How to Pack for a Weekend on Our Coast
settings.excerpt: The weather here changes its mind by the hour, and the guests who enjoy it most are the ones who pack for all of it.
settings.body:
We have welcomed enough weekend guests to know what they wish they had brought. The coast here is beautiful and entirely unpredictable.

The single best thing you can pack is layers and a proper waterproof. A bright morning can turn to sea fog by lunch and back to sun by tea.

What to actually put in the bag

You do not need much, but a few things make all the difference between a soggy weekend and a happy one:

  • Layers you can add and shed as the weather turns.
  • A proper waterproof, not just a light jacket.
  • Shoes you do not mind getting muddy.

Bring shoes you can get muddy. The best walk from our door crosses a field and a shingle beach, and it is no place for white trainers.

A walk worth the wellies

If the weather lets you, take the path from the gate, across the field, and down onto the shingle. It is about an hour each way at an easy pace, and the light on the water late in the afternoon is the thing guests talk about over breakfast the next day. We are happy to point you to the start.

Leave space in the bag for the way home. The smokehouse in the village and the little pottery both have a way of following guests back.
